seoData.title: Claude + ChatGPT Prompts for Health — Expert Templates | Prompt Writing Studio
seoData.description: Copy-paste Claude and ChatGPT prompts for health goals, meal planning, workout routines, mental health check-ins, and medical research summaries.

answerBlock: ChatGPT prompts for health are structured instructions that guide ChatGPT to create personalised meal plans, workout routines, mental health check-ins, and wellness advice. Each prompt below is copy-paste ready and designed to produce specific, actionable health guidance rather than generic responses. They work with ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini.

promptTemplates.title: Personal Fitness Plan Creator
promptTemplates.description: Design a customized workout plan that fits your goals, schedule, and fitness level.
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to create a personalized fitness plan for [specific goal: weight loss, muscle gain, endurance, strength, general health]. I'm [age] years old, [current fitness level: beginner/intermediate/advanced], and can work out [X] days per week for [X] minutes each session. My current fitness includes [list any existing exercise habits or limitations]. Please help me create a comprehensive fitness plan that: 1) Sets realistic, measurable goals with timelines, 2) Includes warm-up and cool-down routines, 3) Provides specific exercises for each muscle group, 4) Incorporates progressive overload principles, 5) Includes cardio and strength training balance, 6) Provides modifications for different fitness levels, 7) Includes recovery and rest day strategies, 8) Addresses nutrition for fitness goals, and 9) Creates a tracking system for progress. Make the plan sustainable and enjoyable for long-term adherence.
promptTemplates.example:
PERSONALIZED FITNESS PLAN

Goal: Build Strength & Muscle
Timeline: 12 weeks
Frequency: 4 days/week
Session Length: 60 minutes

WEEK 1-4: Foundation Building

Day 1: Upper Body Focus
- Warm-up: 10 min dynamic stretching + 5 min cardio
- Push-ups: 3 sets x max reps (with modifications if needed)
- Dumbbell rows: 3 sets x 12 reps each arm
- Overhead press: 3 sets x 10 reps
- Planks: 3 sets x 30 seconds
- Cool-down: 5 min stretching

Day 2: Lower Body Focus
- Warm-up: 10 min dynamic stretching + 5 min cardio
- Bodyweight squats: 3 sets x 15 reps...
tips: Be honest about your current fitness level and limitations
Include any injuries or health conditions
Specify your available equipment and workout space

promptTemplates.title: Nutrition Strategy Developer
promptTemplates.description: Create a personalized nutrition plan that supports your health and fitness goals.
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to develop a nutrition strategy for [specific goal: weight loss, muscle gain, energy optimization, general health, athletic performance]. I'm [age] years old, [current activity level], and have [list any dietary restrictions, allergies, or preferences]. My current eating habits include [describe typical daily meals and snacks]. Please help me create a comprehensive nutrition plan that: 1) Calculates appropriate caloric needs and macronutrient ratios, 2) Provides meal timing strategies for my schedule, 3) Includes specific food recommendations and portion sizes, 4) Addresses hydration needs and timing, 5) Provides meal prep and planning strategies, 6) Includes healthy snack options and alternatives, 7) Addresses eating out and social situations, 8) Provides progress tracking methods, and 9) Includes adjustment strategies as goals change. Focus on sustainable, enjoyable eating patterns.
promptTemplates.example:
PERSONALIZED NUTRITION STRATEGY

Goal: Build Muscle & Strength
Activity Level: Moderate (4-5 workouts/week)
Caloric Needs: 2,200 calories/day

MACRONUTRIENT BREAKDOWN:
- Protein: 165g (30% of calories) - 1g per lb bodyweight
- Carbohydrates: 220g (40% of calories) - Fuel for workouts
- Fats: 73g (30% of calories) - Hormone production & recovery

MEAL TIMING STRATEGY:
- Pre-workout (2-3 hours): Balanced meal with carbs + protein
- Post-workout (within 30 min): Protein + carbs for recovery
- Bedtime: Protein + healthy fats for overnight recovery

SAMPLE MEAL PLAN:
Breakfast (7 AM):
- 3 eggs + 1 cup oatmeal + 1 banana + 1 tbsp almond butter
- Calories: 450 | Protein: 25g | Carbs: 45g | Fat: 18g...
tips: Include any dietary restrictions or preferences
Be honest about your current eating habits
Mention your typical daily schedule and meal timing

promptTemplates.title: Wellness Routine Designer
promptTemplates.description: Create a comprehensive wellness routine that addresses physical, mental, and emotional health.
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to develop a holistic wellness routine that improves my overall health and well-being. I'm currently experiencing [list specific health concerns: stress, poor sleep, low energy, etc.] and have [X] minutes daily to dedicate to wellness activities. My current routine includes [describe existing wellness practices]. Please help me create a comprehensive wellness plan that: 1) Addresses my specific health concerns with targeted solutions, 2) Includes morning and evening routines for optimal energy, 3) Incorporates stress management and mindfulness practices, 4) Provides sleep optimization strategies, 5) Includes movement and exercise recommendations, 6) Addresses nutrition and hydration habits, 7) Provides social connection and community building ideas, 8) Includes progress tracking and habit formation strategies, and 9) Creates a sustainable weekly schedule. Focus on small, manageable changes that compound over time.
promptTemplates.example:
COMPREHENSIVE WELLNESS ROUTINE

MORNING ROUTINE (30 minutes):
6:00 AM - Wake up naturally (no snooze)
6:05 AM - Hydrate with 16 oz water + lemon
6:10 AM - 5 min deep breathing + gratitude practice
6:15 AM - 10 min gentle stretching or yoga
6:25 AM - 10 min planning and intention setting

EVENING ROUTINE (45 minutes):
8:00 PM - Begin wind-down process
8:15 PM - 15 min gentle stretching or foam rolling
8:30 PM - 10 min meditation or journaling
8:40 PM - 10 min reading (no screens)
8:50 PM - 5 min gratitude reflection
9:00 PM - Lights out, sleep preparation

WEEKLY WELLNESS ACTIVITIES:
Monday: Stress management (breathing exercises)
Tuesday: Social connection (call a friend/family member)...
tips: List your specific health concerns and goals
Be realistic about time commitments
Include any existing wellness practices you enjoy

promptTemplates.title: Sleep Optimization Specialist
promptTemplates.description: Improve your sleep quality and quantity through evidence-based strategies.
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm struggling with [specific sleep issues: falling asleep, staying asleep, poor sleep quality, insufficient sleep]. I typically go to bed at [time] and wake up at [time], but I feel [describe how you feel upon waking]. My current sleep environment includes [describe bedroom setup, lighting, noise, temperature]. Please help me create a comprehensive sleep optimization plan that: 1) Identifies potential sleep disruptors in my current routine, 2) Creates an optimal sleep environment setup, 3) Develops a consistent bedtime routine, 4) Addresses pre-sleep habits and activities to avoid, 5) Provides stress management techniques for better sleep, 6) Includes sleep schedule optimization strategies, 7) Addresses nutrition and hydration timing, 8) Provides technology and screen time guidelines, and 9) Creates a sleep tracking and improvement system. Focus on evidence-based strategies that promote natural, restorative sleep.
promptTemplates.example:
SLEEP OPTIMIZATION PLAN

SLEEP ENVIRONMENT OPTIMIZATION:
1. Temperature: 65-68°F (optimal for sleep)
2. Lighting: Complete darkness (blackout curtains, eye mask)
3. Noise: White noise machine or earplugs
4. Bedding: Comfortable, breathable materials
5. Electronics: Remove all devices from bedroom

BEDTIME ROUTINE (1 hour before sleep):
8:00 PM - Begin wind-down process
8:15 PM - Dim lights throughout house
8:30 PM - Take warm bath or shower
8:45 PM - Gentle stretching or yoga
9:00 PM - Reading (physical book, no screens)
9:30 PM - Final bathroom visit
9:45 PM - Deep breathing exercises
10:00 PM - Lights out, sleep

PRE-SLEEP HABITS TO AVOID:
- Caffeine after 2 PM
- Large meals within 3 hours of bedtime...
tips: Be specific about your sleep issues and schedule
Describe your current sleep environment honestly
Include any factors that might affect your sleep

promptTemplates.title: Stress Management Strategist
promptTemplates.description: Develop effective stress management techniques for better mental and physical health.
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm experiencing [describe specific stress symptoms: anxiety, overwhelm, irritability, physical tension, etc.] due to [list stress sources: work, relationships, health, finances, etc.]. I have [X] minutes daily to dedicate to stress management and prefer [list any preferred activities: meditation, exercise, nature, creative pursuits, etc.]. My current stress management includes [describe existing coping mechanisms]. Please help me create a comprehensive stress management plan that: 1) Identifies my stress triggers and patterns, 2) Provides immediate stress relief techniques (5 minutes or less), 3) Creates daily stress prevention practices, 4) Includes weekly stress reduction activities, 5) Addresses lifestyle factors that contribute to stress, 6) Provides boundary-setting and communication strategies, 7) Includes professional help recommendations if needed, 8) Creates a stress tracking and management system, and 9) Provides long-term stress resilience building. Focus on practical, sustainable techniques that fit my lifestyle.
promptTemplates.example:
COMPREHENSIVE STRESS MANAGEMENT PLAN

STRESS TRIGGER IDENTIFICATION:
1. Work deadlines and pressure
2. Financial concerns
3. Relationship conflicts
4. Health worries
5. Information overload

IMMEDIATE STRESS RELIEF (5 minutes or less):
1. Box Breathing: 4 counts in, hold 4, 4 counts out, hold 4
2. Progressive Muscle Relaxation: Tense and release each muscle group
3. 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding: Name 5 things you see, 4 you feel, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste
4. Quick Walk: 5-minute walk around the block or office
5. Music Therapy: Listen to favorite calming song

DAILY STRESS PREVENTION:
Morning: 10 min meditation or journaling
Midday: 5 min breathing break every 2 hours
Evening: 15 min decompression activity...
tips: Be honest about your stress sources and symptoms
Include any preferred stress management activities
Mention your daily schedule and time constraints

promptTemplates.title: Meal Planning Assistant
promptTemplates.description: Build a week of healthy, balanced meals around your dietary needs and schedule.
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a 7-day meal plan for [dietary approach: Mediterranean, low-carb, plant-based, balanced]. I need approximately [X] calories per day, have [list any allergies or intolerances], and have [X] minutes for cooking on weekdays and [X] minutes on weekends. Please include: 1) Breakfast, lunch, dinner, and two snacks for each day, 2) Macro breakdown per meal (protein, carbs, fats, calories), 3) A consolidated shopping list organised by category, 4) Meal prep tips to save time, 5) Simple swaps for days when I have less time, and 6) How to adjust portions if I need more or fewer calories. Prioritise whole foods, minimise processed ingredients, and keep meals enjoyable.
promptTemplates.example:
7-DAY MEAL PLAN (2,000 calories/day, Mediterranean)

MONDAY
Breakfast: Greek yogurt parfait — 350 kcal | P:20g C:40g F:10g
Lunch: Chickpea & roasted veg salad with tahini — 450 kcal | P:15g C:50g F:18g
Dinner: Baked salmon, quinoa, steamed broccoli — 550 kcal | P:40g C:45g F:18g
Snack 1: Apple + almond butter — 200 kcal
Snack 2: Hummus & carrot sticks — 150 kcal

SHOPPING LIST
Produce: spinach, tomatoes, cucumber, broccoli, bell peppers...
tips: List all allergies and intolerances upfront
Specify whether you prefer to cook in bulk or fresh each day
Mention your cooking skill level so recipes are appropriate

promptTemplates.title: Mental Health Check-In Companion
promptTemplates.description: Process emotions, identify patterns, and build better mental health habits with a structured journalling prompt.
promptTemplates.prompt: Act as a compassionate mental wellness guide. Help me complete a structured mental health check-in for today. I want to reflect on: 1) My current emotional state and what triggered it, 2) Physical sensations I notice in my body, 3) Any recurring thoughts or worries, 4) What went well today and why, 5) What was challenging and how I responded, 6) One thing I can do differently tomorrow, and 7) A specific self-care action I'll take tonight. Ask me each question one at a time, acknowledge my answer with empathy, and help me identify any patterns or insights. End with a brief affirmation based on what I've shared. Note: This is for personal reflection only, not a substitute for professional mental health care.
promptTemplates.example:
Mental Health Check-In — [Date]

Emotional State: Anxious and slightly overwhelmed
Trigger: Back-to-back meetings with no breaks

Body Sensations: Tension in shoulders, slight headache, shallow breathing

Recurring Thoughts: 'I have too much to do' / 'I'm not keeping up'

What Went Well: Completed the project draft despite interruptions

Challenge & Response: Felt irritable at 3pm — took a 5-min walk instead of snapping

Tomorrow's Intention: Schedule a 10-min buffer between morning meetings

Self-Care Tonight: 20-min yoga and no screens after 9pm

Insight: Your nervous system needs recovery time as much as your mind does...
tips: Do this check-in at the same time each day for best results
Be honest — there are no wrong answers
Note any patterns across multiple days to spot trends

promptTemplates.title: Supplement Research Evaluator
promptTemplates.description: Get an evidence-based evaluation of any supplement before you buy.
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm considering taking [supplement name] for [specific goal: sleep, energy, muscle recovery, immune support, etc.]. Please provide a research-based evaluation that covers: 1) What the current scientific evidence says about its effectiveness for my goal, 2) Typical dosage ranges used in studies versus common product doses, 3) Known side effects, contraindications, and drug interactions, 4) Quality markers to look for when choosing a product (third-party testing, forms, bioavailability), 5) How long it typically takes to see results if effective, 6) Lifestyle factors that may work as well or better, and 7) When I should consult a doctor before starting. Use plain language and cite the strength of evidence (strong, moderate, limited) for each claim.
promptTemplates.example:
SUPPLEMENT EVALUATION: Magnesium Glycinate for Sleep

Evidence Strength: Moderate

Effectiveness: Studies show magnesium supplementation improves sleep onset latency and subjective sleep quality, particularly in people with deficiency. Effect size is modest for healthy individuals.

Dosage: 200–400mg elemental magnesium/day in studies. Look for magnesium glycinate or malate (better absorbed than oxide).

Side Effects: Generally well tolerated. High doses may cause loose stools. Avoid if you have kidney disease.

Drug Interactions: May interact with antibiotics, diuretics, and diabetes medications — check with your pharmacist...
tips: Include any medications you take to check for interactions
Specify whether you want plant-based or animal-derived options
Mention any existing health conditions

promptTemplates.title: Injury Recovery Protocol Builder
promptTemplates.description: Create a safe, phased return-to-activity plan after an injury.
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm recovering from [specific injury or condition] and was cleared by my doctor/physio to start gentle rehabilitation [X] weeks ago. My current pain level is [0–10] and I can perform [describe current movement limitations]. Please help me build a phased recovery plan that: 1) Outlines safe activities for my current stage, 2) Provides a week-by-week progression schedule, 3) Lists warning signs that mean I should stop and consult my doctor, 4) Includes complementary strategies like ice/heat, compression, and elevation timing, 5) Recommends nutrition and sleep habits that support tissue healing, 6) Suggests when I can realistically return to [specific activity or sport], and 7) Includes mental strategies for staying patient during recovery. Emphasise safety over speed of recovery.
promptTemplates.example:
ANKLE SPRAIN RECOVERY PLAN (Grade II, Week 3)

Current Clearances: Weight-bearing with mild discomfort, no running

WEEK 3–4: Stability Foundation
- Single-leg standing: 3x30 seconds each leg
- Resistance band dorsiflexion/plantarflexion: 3x15
- Calf raises (bilateral): 3x15
- Swimming or pool walking: 20 min 3x/week

STOP and consult physio if: Sharp pain above 4/10, swelling increases, any instability during standing

Nutrition support: Increase vitamin C (collagen synthesis), zinc (tissue repair), adequate protein (1.6g/kg/day)...
tips: Always follow your medical professional's specific guidance over general advice
Include the exact injury and any complications your doctor mentioned
Be honest about your current pain levels and limitations

promptTemplates.title: Habit Tracker & Accountability Coach
promptTemplates.description: Design a realistic habit-stacking system to build lasting health routines.
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to build the following health habits: [list 3–5 habits you want to develop, e.g. daily exercise, drinking 2L water, 7+ hours sleep, daily meditation]. My current routine is [describe your typical weekday and weekend schedule]. I've tried and failed at these habits before because [explain what went wrong]. Please help me create a sustainable habit system by: 1) Stacking new habits onto existing anchors (morning coffee, lunch break, etc.), 2) Designing a minimum viable version of each habit for low-energy days, 3) Creating environmental design tips to make habits easier, 4) Building a simple daily tracking system I'll actually use, 5) Planning for obstacle scenarios (travel, late nights, illness), 6) Setting a realistic 30/60/90-day progression plan, and 7) Identifying the single most impactful habit to start with first. Prioritise consistency over perfection.
promptTemplates.example:
HABIT SYSTEM DESIGN

Anchor Habit: Morning coffee

HABIT STACK:
7:00 — Make coffee → 5 min stretching while it brews (anchor: coffee)
7:10 — Drink coffee → Journal 3 gratitudes (anchor: coffee drinking)
12:30 — Lunch break → 10-min walk outside (anchor: lunch)
21:30 — Brush teeth → Drink final glass of water (anchor: teeth)

MINIMUM VIABLE VERSIONS:
- Exercise: 10 push-ups (on low-energy days)
- Hydration: 1 extra glass with each meal
- Sleep: Phones out of bedroom by 10pm

Environmental design: Fill water bottle the night before and leave on desk...
tips: Be specific about what has made habits fail in the past
List your existing daily anchors (wake time, meals, commute, routines)
Start with 1–2 habits maximum before adding more

promptTemplates.title: Medical Research Summariser
promptTemplates.description: Translate complex medical information into clear, actionable summaries.
promptTemplates.prompt: I need help understanding [medical topic, condition, treatment, or test result]. Please provide a clear, jargon-free explanation that covers: 1) What this condition or treatment is in plain language, 2) The most common causes or risk factors, 3) Standard diagnostic criteria or what tests are typically used, 4) Evidence-based treatment options (from most to least common), 5) Lifestyle factors that can improve or worsen the condition, 6) Questions I should ask my doctor at my next appointment, 7) Reliable resources for further reading (major medical organisations only), and 8) What red flag symptoms require urgent medical attention. Write for someone with no medical background. Always note that this is for general information only and does not replace professional medical advice.
promptTemplates.example:
TOPIC: Type 2 Diabetes — Plain Language Summary

What it is: Your body can't use insulin properly, causing blood sugar to build up. Over time this damages blood vessels, nerves, and organs.

Risk factors: Excess weight (especially belly fat), physical inactivity, family history, age 45+, previously high blood sugar

Diagnosis: HbA1c test (measures 3-month average blood sugar). Diabetes: ≥6.5%. Pre-diabetes: 5.7–6.4%

Treatment options: Lifestyle changes first (diet + exercise), then Metformin, then other medications if needed

Questions for your doctor: What's my target HbA1c? Should I see a dietitian? Do I need to monitor at home?...
tips: Include any test results or numbers you've been given
Mention medications you currently take
Note whether you want information about conventional, complementary, or both approaches

promptTemplates.title: Sleep Debt Recovery Plan
promptTemplates.description: Calculate your sleep debt and build a realistic recovery strategy.
promptTemplates.prompt: I've been [describe sleep situation: averaging X hours/night for the past Y weeks/months, dealing with insomnia, jet lag, shift work, newborn disruptions, etc.]. My ideal sleep amount is approximately [X] hours but I'm currently getting [X] hours. I feel [describe daytime symptoms: fatigue, brain fog, mood changes, poor concentration]. Please help me: 1) Estimate my current sleep debt and realistic recovery timeline, 2) Create a safe catch-up strategy (naps vs. extended night sleep), 3) Identify the highest-impact changes to my sleep environment, 4) Design a wind-down routine that suits my schedule, 5) Address specific disruptors I mentioned, 6) Explain which symptoms should prompt a doctor visit, and 7) Provide a 2-week recovery schedule I can follow. Base advice on sleep science, not just tips.
promptTemplates.example:
SLEEP RECOVERY ASSESSMENT

Estimated debt: ~14 hours (2 hrs/night deficit x 7 nights)
Recovery timeline: 2–3 weeks of consistent full-night sleep

RECOVERY STRATEGY:
Week 1: Target 8.5 hrs in bed (30 min more than usual)
Week 2: Maintain 8 hrs + one 20-min nap before 3pm if needed
Week 3: Return to normal 7.5-hr schedule if symptoms resolve

Do NOT: Sleep in more than 1 extra hour on weekends (disrupts circadian rhythm)

WIND-DOWN ROUTINE (60 min):
9:00pm — Dim lights, stop eating
9:20pm — Warm shower
9:40pm — Read paper book or journalling
10:00pm — Lights out

See a doctor if: Fatigue persists after 3 weeks of good sleep, you stop breathing at night, or mood symptoms worsen...
tips: Track your actual sleep for one week before starting the plan
Include any stress or anxiety factors affecting your sleep
Mention your work schedule and when you must wake up

promptTemplates.title: Preventive Health Checklist Builder
promptTemplates.description: Generate a personalised schedule of health screenings and preventive care based on your age, sex, and risk factors.
promptTemplates.prompt: I am [age] years old, [biological sex], with the following health background: [list any diagnosed conditions, family history of major diseases, smoking status, BMI range, activity level]. Please create a personalised preventive health checklist that includes: 1) Recommended health screenings with frequency (blood pressure, cholesterol, cancer screenings, etc.), 2) Vaccines I may need as an adult, 3) Dental and eye care schedule, 4) Mental health check-in recommendations, 5) Lifestyle risk factors I should discuss with my doctor, 6) What to track between doctor visits (metrics, symptoms, logs), and 7) Red flag symptoms that should prompt an unscheduled doctor visit. Base recommendations on major health authority guidelines (NHS, CDC, or equivalent) and note where recommendations differ by individual risk.
promptTemplates.example:
PREVENTIVE HEALTH PLAN — 45-year-old Female

SCREENINGS
Every visit: Blood pressure, weight/BMI
Annually: Blood glucose, cholesterol panel, thyroid (if symptoms)
Every 2 years: Skin check (dermatologist, higher risk)
Age 40+: Mammogram every 1–2 years
Age 45+: Colorectal cancer screening

VACCINES
Flu: Annually
Tdap booster: Every 10 years
Shingles (Shingrix): Ages 50+ (2 doses)
COVID-19: Per current guidance

DENTAL: Every 6 months
EYE: Every 2 years (annually if wearing glasses/contacts)

Discuss with your doctor: Family history of breast cancer (BRCA testing?), perimenopause symptoms...
tips: Include family history of heart disease, cancer, diabetes, or mental health conditions
Mention your country so guidelines can be region-appropriate
Note any screenings you've already completed and when

promptTemplates.title: Post-Workout Recovery Optimizer
promptTemplates.description: Design a science-backed recovery protocol for your training type and goals.
promptTemplates.prompt: I just completed [describe workout: type, duration, intensity level, muscle groups targeted]. My goal is [muscle gain / fat loss / endurance / general health] and I train [X] days per week. I currently do the following for recovery: [describe current post-workout routine]. Please create an optimised recovery protocol covering: 1) The ideal post-workout nutrition window and what to eat, 2) Hydration and electrolyte replenishment strategy, 3) Active recovery options vs. complete rest (with criteria for choosing), 4) Targeted stretching or mobility work for the muscles I trained, 5) Sleep quality tips specifically for recovery nights, 6) Supplements with evidence for recovery (if any), and 7) How to tell if I'm under-recovering (signs to watch for). Focus on practical, immediately actionable steps.
promptTemplates.example:
POST-WORKOUT RECOVERY: Heavy Leg Day

NUTRITION (within 30–45 min):
- Protein: 30–40g (chicken, Greek yogurt, protein shake)
- Carbs: 50–70g (rice, sweet potato, banana) to replenish glycogen
- Avoid high-fat meals immediately post-workout (slows absorption)

HYDRATION:
- Replace 150% of fluid lost: weigh before/after or use urine colour guide
- Add electrolytes if session exceeded 60 min or was in heat

MOBILITY (10–15 min):
- Hip flexor stretch: 60 sec each side
- Pigeon pose: 90 sec each side
- Foam roll quads and IT band: 60 sec each

SLEEP: Target 8+ hrs tonight. Avoid alcohol (disrupts REM + muscle protein synthesis)...
tips: Note whether your goal is strength, hypertrophy, endurance, or fat loss
Include your training frequency so recovery advice is appropriate
Mention any supplements you already take

promptTemplates.title: Chronic Condition Daily Management Guide
promptTemplates.description: Build a sustainable daily management system for living well with a chronic health condition.
promptTemplates.prompt: I have been diagnosed with [chronic condition: e.g., Type 2 diabetes, hypertension, hypothyroidism, IBS, chronic pain, autoimmune condition]. My current management includes [medications, treatments, lifestyle changes already in place]. I struggle most with [describe your biggest challenges: adherence, energy, symptoms, social situations, etc.]. Please help me design a daily management system that: 1) Structures my day to minimise symptom triggers and maximise energy, 2) Creates simple monitoring routines (what to track, how often), 3) Designs meal timing and content guidelines specific to my condition, 4) Builds movement and rest patterns that work with my symptoms, 5) Prepares me for high-symptom days with a contingency plan, 6) Identifies when to contact my healthcare team versus manage at home, and 7) Addresses the emotional and mental health aspects of chronic illness. Personalise to my specific condition and note when professional guidance is essential.
promptTemplates.example:
DAILY MANAGEMENT: Type 2 Diabetes

MORNING ROUTINE:
7:00 — Check blood glucose (target: 80–130 mg/dL fasting)
7:15 — Medication with breakfast (if prescribed)
7:30 — Breakfast: protein + fibre first, carbs last (reduces glucose spike)

MONITORING:
- Blood glucose: before meals + 2 hrs after largest meal
- Blood pressure: weekly (if also hypertensive)
- Weight: weekly, same time, same conditions

HIGH-SYMPTOM DAY PLAN (glucose >180 or <70):
- Above 180: Light walk, increase water, check for illness or stress
- Below 70: 15g fast carbs immediately, retest in 15 min

Contact healthcare team if: Glucose consistently above 250, any hypoglycaemic episode below 54, new symptoms...
tips: Include your exact diagnosis and any comorbidities
List your current medications and any known triggers
Note whether you have a specialist or only a GP managing your care

useCases.title: Health Enthusiasts & Fitness Buffs
useCases.description: Health-conscious individuals use these prompts to create personalized fitness plans, nutrition strategies, and wellness routines that optimize their physical and mental health.

useCases.title: Wellness Coaches & Trainers
useCases.description: Health professionals use these prompts to develop comprehensive wellness plans, create client programs, and provide evidence-based health recommendations.

useCases.title: Busy Professionals
useCases.description: Working professionals use these prompts to optimize their health despite busy schedules, manage stress, and maintain energy levels for peak performance.

sections.heading: Step-by-Step Guide to Writing ChatGPT Health Prompts
body: The quality of your ChatGPT health output depends almost entirely on how well you structure your prompt. A vague request like 'help me get healthy' produces generic advice. A specific, well-structured prompt produces a plan you can actually follow. These seven steps will take you from vague question to actionable health guidance.

items.term: Step 1 — Define your specific health topic
items.description: Choose one area: nutrition, fitness, sleep, stress management, mental health, or a chronic condition. Broad prompts produce broad answers. 'Help me improve my diet' is less useful than 'Help me build a low-sugar meal plan for a type 2 diabetic.'

items.term: Step 2 — Set the role and context
items.description: Tell ChatGPT who to be: 'Act as a certified nutritionist with experience working with endurance athletes.' Role assignment activates relevant knowledge and adjusts the tone and depth of the response.

Add age, activity level, dietary restrictions, health goals, and any relevant medical context. The more specific your input, the more relevant the output. You do not need to share identifying details — general parameters are enough.

Tell ChatGPT how to structure the output: 'Give me a 7-day plan in table format', 'Keep each recommendation under two sentences', or 'Use plain language, no medical jargon.' Format instructions prevent padding and improve usability.

items.term: Step 5 — Use follow-up prompts to refine
items.description: The first response is a starting point, not the final answer. Follow up: 'Make the meal plan more budget-friendly', 'Add vegetarian options for Tuesdays', or 'Simplify the workout routine for someone with knee pain.'

items.term: Step 6 — Cross-reference outputs with trusted sources
items.description: Always verify health information against authoritative sources: the HSE (Ireland), NHS (UK), WHO, or peer-reviewed research on PubMed. AI health content is a useful starting point, not a substitute for professional guidance.

items.term: Step 7 — Save and iterate on what works
items.description: Keep a document of your best-performing health prompts. When a prompt produces a particularly useful output, save the exact wording. Over time you build a personal prompt library that consistently delivers the health guidance you need.

sections.heading: Best Tools to Pair with ChatGPT for Health Prompts
body: ChatGPT works best as part of a broader health toolkit. These complementary tools make your AI health workflow more accurate, trackable, and actionable.

items.description: The paid tier is worth it for health tasks — GPT-4o handles nuanced health scenarios more carefully, follows complex constraint lists more precisely, and is less likely to give oversimplified advice.

Use Perplexity to cross-check health claims from ChatGPT with cited sources. It searches the live web and cites PubMed, NHS, and WHO documents — making it ideal for verifying specific health claims or medication information.

Track your actual nutrition data in one of these apps and feed real numbers into your ChatGPT prompts: 'I averaged 1,800 calories and 60g protein last week. Help me adjust my plan to reach 120g protein without exceeding 2,000 calories.'
Build a personal health prompt library in a note-taking app. Store your best prompts, tag them by health topic, and add notes on what worked and what needed refining.

For health topics requiring careful, nuanced responses, Claude tends to follow constraint lists more precisely than ChatGPT and is more willing to flag uncertainty. Useful when you want the AI to explicitly say what it does not know.

sections.heading: Advanced Prompting Techniques for Health Content
body: Once you have mastered the basics, these techniques unlock significantly better health outputs from ChatGPT.

items.description: Ask ChatGPT to reason through a health decision step by step before giving a recommendation: 'Think through the pros and cons of intermittent fasting for someone with a history of disordered eating before giving your recommendation.' This produces more considered, nuanced responses.

Show ChatGPT two or three examples of the output format you want before asking for new content. Paste in an example meal plan or workout structure and say 'Create a new plan following exactly this format.'

Break a complex health goal into a sequence of prompts, each building on the last. First generate a health assessment, then use that output to generate a meal plan, then use the meal plan to generate a shopping list.

If you use the ChatGPT API or Custom Instructions, set a persistent health persona: 'You are a qualified fitness coach specialising in postpartum recovery. Always prioritise safety, always recommend consulting a GP for medical concerns.' This applies to every conversation automatically.

Add to any health prompt: 'Flag any claims you are less than highly confident about with [UNCERTAIN]. List three things I should verify with a doctor before acting on this advice.' This makes the AI's confidence level visible.

sections.heading: Important Disclaimers: Using AI for Health Responsibly
body: AI-generated health content is not medical advice and should never replace consultation with a qualified healthcare professional. ChatGPT does not know your full medical history, cannot examine you, and can make errors — particularly on topics involving medications, dosages, diagnoses, or conditions with serious health implications.

Always verify AI health outputs against authoritative sources: the HSE (hse.ie) for Irish users, the NHS (nhs.uk) for UK users, the WHO (who.int) for international guidance, and PubMed for peer-reviewed research. For anything involving symptoms, medications, or an existing diagnosis, bring AI-generated plans to your GP or specialist before acting on them.

On data privacy: avoid sharing personally identifiable health information with third-party AI tools. Use general parameters ('a 45-year-old with Type 2 diabetes') rather than your name and specific details. Check each tool's data retention policy and, for GDPR compliance, confirm whether your data is stored or used for model training. Irish users are protected by GDPR — you have the right to request deletion of any stored data.

internalLinks.anchor: how to write effective AI prompts

faqs.question: Can ChatGPT give accurate health advice?
faqs.answer: ChatGPT can provide useful general health information, explain medical concepts, help plan meals and fitness routines, and summarise research — but it is not a medical professional. It does not know your full health history, cannot examine you, and can make errors on complex or nuanced health topics. Always verify outputs with trusted sources (HSE, NHS, WHO, PubMed) and consult a doctor for any personal health concerns, symptoms, or decisions about medications and treatments.

faqs.question: Is it safe to use ChatGPT for health questions?
faqs.answer: It is safe for general research, wellness planning, and health education. ChatGPT is useful for building meal plans, understanding medical terminology, creating fitness routines, and exploring evidence-based wellness strategies. However, it should never replace professional medical consultation — especially for serious symptoms, medications, diagnoses, or mental health crises. Treat AI health outputs as a starting point for informed conversations with your healthcare provider, not as final medical guidance.

faqs.question: What are the best ChatGPT prompts for meal planning?
faqs.answer: Effective meal planning prompts include: your dietary approach (Mediterranean, low-carb, plant-based), daily calorie target, any allergies or intolerances, your cooking skill level, how much time you have to cook, and the number of people you are cooking for. The more specific your input, the more tailored and immediately usable the meal plan. Use the Meal Planning Assistant prompt on this page as a starting template and fill in your specific details.

faqs.question: Can ChatGPT create personalised fitness plans?
faqs.answer: Yes. When given your current fitness level, specific goal (weight loss, muscle gain, endurance), available equipment, workout frequency, and any injuries or limitations, ChatGPT can generate structured, progressive workout routines. The quality of the plan scales directly with the specificity of your input. Use the Personal Fitness Plan Creator prompt above, fill in every placeholder, and follow up with questions to refine the plan for your specific situation.

faqs.question: How do I make ChatGPT health prompts more accurate?
faqs.answer: Assign a specific expert role (certified nutritionist, physiotherapist, sleep specialist), provide detailed personal context (age, activity level, health history), include explicit constraints (no processed food, exercises for limited mobility), and ask ChatGPT to flag any claims it is uncertain about. Always follow up by cross-referencing the output with authoritative health sources before acting on the advice.

faqs.question: Should I share personal health data with ChatGPT?
faqs.answer: Avoid sharing personally identifiable health information. Use general parameters instead — 'a 45-year-old with Type 2 diabetes' rather than your name and specific details. Check each tool's data retention policy before use. Under GDPR (which applies to Irish and EU users), you have the right to request deletion of any stored data. For sensitive health conditions, consider using ChatGPT's Privacy Mode or Anthropic's Claude with data retention turned off.
